question: What is the main difference between makeAutoObservable and makeObservable?

options.text: makeAutoObservable automatically infers annotations, makeObservable requires explicit ones
makeAutoObservable works only with classes, makeObservable — only with objects
options.explanation: Both methods work with classes. makeObservable can also be used with plain objects, but the key difference is automatic vs manual annotation specification.

options.text: makeAutoObservable is faster in terms of performance
options.explanation: There is no runtime performance difference. The only difference is convenience: makeAutoObservable infers annotations automatically.

options.text: makeAutoObservable supports TypeScript, but makeObservable does not
Only full reference replacement, not mutations inside the object
All changes to nested object properties
options.explanation: Deep tracking is the behavior of regular observable (default). observable.ref only tracks reference replacement.

options.text: Only the first level of object nesting
options.explanation: Shallow first-level tracking is observable.shallow, not observable.ref.
observable.ref is not related to DOM. ref means 'reference tracking' — tracking object reference replacement.

question: Why won't properties added after calling makeAutoObservable be observable?

options.text: makeAutoObservable registers only properties that exist at the time of the call
JavaScript doesn't allow adding new properties to a class
options.explanation: JavaScript allows dynamically adding properties. The issue is that MobX won't know about them because registration already happened.

options.text: You need to call makeAutoObservable again
options.explanation: makeAutoObservable cannot be called again for the same object. All observable properties must be declared before the call.

options.text: This is a MobX bug that's been fixed in newer versions
options.explanation: This is not a bug but a design decision. MobX works with existing object properties at the time makeAutoObservable is called.

question: Which collections can MobX make observable by default?

options.explanation: MobX doesn't support WeakMap as observable because WeakMap is not an enumerable collection — you can't track which keys it contains.
MobX doesn't support WeakSet as observable for the same reason — WeakSet is not enumerable.
